# Koan Framework — agent retrieval map

Koan is an opinionated .NET 10 meta-framework for agentic, data-driven applications. Application code
states business intent; capability pillars own composition, provider negotiation, lifecycle, and
explanation.

Current line: 0.20 preview. Use the generated product surface at
docs/reference/product-surface.md as the sole maturity and supported-package authority. Package
presence, repository proximity, or historical documentation does not imply support.

## Canonical application grammar

Install and create:

    dotnet new install Sylin.Koan.Templates
    dotnet new koan-web -o TodoApi

Host:

    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
    builder.Services.AddKoan();
    var app = builder.Build();
    await app.RunAsync();

Entity and HTTP projection:

    public sealed class Todo : Entity<Todo>;
    [Route("api/todos")]
    public sealed class TodosController : EntityController<Todo>;

Entity operations:

    await new Todo().Save();
    var todo = await Todo.Get(id);
    var open = await Todo.Query(item => !item.Done);
    await todo.Remove();

References make capabilities available. AddKoan() compiles referenced semantic modules once. A
capability elects providers and rejects unsupported configured intent with a corrective explanation.
Do not add provider registration, framework service locators, repositories around ordinary Entity
operations, or manual endpoint mapping unless the owning capability explicitly documents an escape
hatch.

## Intent-to-document map

- Start or orient: README.md, docs/getting-started/quickstart.md, docs/index.md
- Add Koan to an existing ASP.NET Core app: docs/getting-started/adopt-existing-app.md
- Foundation/composition/configuration: docs/reference/core/index.md
- Entity persistence/query/providers: docs/reference/data/index.md
- HTTP/API/OpenAPI/SSE: docs/reference/web/index.md
- Authentication/authorization/tenancy: docs/reference/identity/index.md
- Jobs/Events/Transport: docs/reference/work/index.md
- Jobs: docs/reference/jobs/index.md
- Entity events and snapshot transport: docs/reference/communication/index.md
- Cache/Storage/Media: docs/reference/state-content/index.md
- AI/model operations: docs/reference/ai/index.md
- Entity embeddings and vector search: docs/reference/ai/vector.md
- MCP/agent tools/resources/transports: docs/reference/agents/index.md
- Canonical Entity pipelines: docs/reference/canon/index.md
- Testing/health/facts/diagnostics: docs/reference/operations/index.md
- OpenTelemetry export: docs/reference/operations/observability.md
- External infrastructure boundary: docs/reference/operations/external-topology.md
- Architecture and adoption decisions: docs/architecture/index.md
- Supported claims and package IDs: docs/reference/product-surface.md
- Complete executable examples: samples/README.md

## Page contract

Prefer the page that owns the capability. Read, in order:

1. business need and when to use it;
2. package/reference prerequisite;
3. smallest public expression;
4. guarantee and provider limits;
5. configuration and election;
6. inspection and corrective failure;
7. package-specific or technical links.

Package README.md files own package-specific installation, configuration, and limits. TECHNICAL.md
files are for lifecycle, internal contracts, extension points, and operations; they are not a second
greenfield path.

## Runtime truth

- Read startup reporting before guessing about composition.
- Use /health/live for process liveness and /health/ready for dependency readiness.
- /.well-known/Koan/facts and koan://facts project the same redacted runtime decisions.
- koan://entities and koan://self describe the agent-visible surface.
- koan.lock.json records referenced-module composition and drift.

## Source precedence and safety

Current capability pages, generated product truth, package companions, graduated samples, source, and
focused tests outrank initiative prose. ADRs, initiatives, assessments, proposals, and archives are
historical engineering evidence.

Never copy private downstream names, paths, URLs, organizations, domain details, data, or recognizable
workflows into public artifacts. Convert observations into anonymous repository-owned reproductions.
